name: Raziel
description: Raziel, whose name translates to "Secret of God", is a prominent angel within Jewish mystical traditions, particularly Kabbalah. He is often described as the angel of secrets and mysteries, possessing profound knowledge of the universe and its workings. Raziel is believed to stand closest to God, privy to divine wisdom and understanding that he diligently records in a sacred book. This book, known as the Sefer Raziel HaMalakh (The Book of Raziel the Angel), is said to contain all the knowledge of heaven and earth, including insights into creation, astrology, magic, and the names and powers of other angels. According to some traditions, Raziel gave this book to Adam after his expulsion from the Garden of Eden, assisting him in understanding the divine plan and finding his way back to God. The book subsequently passed down through generations to figures like Enoch and Noah, becoming a source of immense power and wisdom for those who possessed it. Raziel's role extends beyond mere knowledge; he is also associated with inspiration, creativity, and the unlocking of hidden potential within individuals.
subtitle: Angel of Secrets
titles: Keeper of the Book of Raziel

keyMyths.title: The Gift to Adam
keyMyths.description: After Adam and Eve were expelled from the Garden of Eden, they were lost and confused. Raziel, in an act of compassion, gifted Adam the Sefer Raziel HaMalakh. This book contained the secrets of the universe, including the knowledge of how to connect with the divine and understand the workings of creation. It helped Adam and his descendants navigate the world and seek reconciliation with God, offering a path towards understanding and spiritual growth. The book became a cherished heirloom, passed down through generations.

keyMyths.title: Raziel's Proximity to God
keyMyths.description: Raziel's position as the angel closest to God allows him access to the most profound secrets of the cosmos. He is privy to the divine counsel and understands the intricate web of creation. It is said that Raziel stands at the edge of the divine realm, constantly listening to the decrees and plans of God. He meticulously records these secrets in his book, ensuring that this vital knowledge is preserved and made available to those who are worthy and prepared to receive it. His role ensures the continuity of divine wisdom.

keyMyths.title: The Book's Power and Peril
keyMyths.description: The Sefer Raziel HaMalakh is not merely a collection of information; it is a conduit of immense power. Its contents are so potent that it is said to radiate a blinding light and possess the ability to shape reality. However, this power comes with great responsibility, and the book is not meant for the uninitiated. Mishandling or misinterpreting its secrets can lead to disastrous consequences. Only those with pure intentions and a deep understanding of Kabbalistic principles can safely access its wisdom.

sources.author: Gershom Scholem
sources.description: A seminal work on the history and development of Jewish mysticism, including discussions of Kabbalistic angelology.
sources.text: A seminal work on the history and development of Jewish mysticism, including discussions of Kabbalistic angelology.

sources.author: Pinchas Giller
sources.work: Reading the Zohar: The Sacred Text of the Kabbalah
sources.description: Provides an analysis of the Zohar, a central text in Kabbalah, and its portrayal of angels and divine secrets.
sources.text: Provides an analysis of the Zohar, a central text in Kabbalah, and its portrayal of angels and divine secrets.

sources.author: Moses Cordovero
sources.description: Details the qualities and attributes of angels, including Raziel, within the framework of Kabbalistic thought.
